title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: A dazzling novel about all the choices that go into a life well lived, set between life and death in a magical library of infinite possibilities.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches programmers to write readable, maintainable, and elegant code through practical examples.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An iconic exploration of the universe's biggest questions — from the Big Bang to black holes — written for curious minds without a physics background.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A mystical story of Santiago, a shepherd boy, who embarks on a journey to find a worldly treasure and discovers the true riches within himself.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: A timeless guide filled with practical advice for software developers, covering topics from personal responsibility to career development and coding techniques.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of humanity's creation and evolution, exploring how biology and history shaped who we are and what we value today.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A Pulitzer Prize-winning masterpiece about racial injustice and moral growth in the American South, seen through the innocent eyes of young Scout Finch.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of JavaScript, helping developers truly understand the language rather than just use it superficially.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking work in evolutionary biology that introduces the gene-centered view of evolution and coins the concept of the 'meme'.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society where Big Brother watches every move, exploring themes of surveillance, truth, and freedom.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: An essential guide for engineers building scalable and reliable systems, covering databases, distributed systems, and data processing in depth.

title: The Gene: An Intimate History
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A compelling history of genetics from Mendel's peas to CRISPR, examining how the gene shapes identity, destiny, and the future of medicine.
